path.name: AI Software Architect
path.description: Foundation path. Build first with AI, then dissect the architecture, repeat until the pattern is internalized. Every other path assumes these skills.
path.targetAudience: Beginners to intermediate developers who want to work fluently with AI coding agents.

programs.name: AI-Assisted Development Foundations
programs.description: Build a working single-room Screeps economy bot (spawn, harvest, build, upgrade) using an AI coding agent (Codex/Claude Code/Gemini CLI) before understanding syntax. Screeps is a real JavaScript programming game — you write code that controls persistent units in a live world — so 'working' means the bot actually runs and survives.
programs.learningOutcomes: Prompt an AI coding agent effectively; describe a system in plain language; iterate on AI output based on real feedback from a running bot.
programs.projectSummary: Single-room Screeps bot: spawns creeps, assigns harvester/upgrader/builder roles, sustains itself for 24 hours.
programs.portfolioArtifact: A live Screeps bot running a basic room economy.

sessions.name: Session 1: Meet Your AI Coding Agent
sessions.objectives: Set up a Screeps account and room; use an AI coding agent to write a first working creep spawn and harvest script.
sessions.liveActivity: Instructor demos prompting an AI agent to spawn a creep and harvest energy; students mirror it live in their own room.
sessions.homework: Create a free Screeps account and claim a starting room before next session.

sessions.name: Session 2: Describing a Full System
sessions.objectives: Describe a full system (spawn, harvest, upgrade, build) in plain language and have the AI implement it end-to-end.
sessions.liveActivity: Students write a plain-English spec for a 3-role economy (harvester/upgrader/builder), then prompt their AI agent to build it.
sessions.homework: Let the bot run overnight; screenshot the room state the next morning.

sessions.name: Session 3: Iterating on Real Feedback
sessions.objectives: Practice iterative prompting: adjust spawn logic based on real room needs.
sessions.liveActivity: Review each bot's overnight behavior; prompt the AI to fix one real issue observed.
sessions.homework: Log 3 bugs or weird behaviors observed in the overnight run.

sessions.name: Session 4: Basic State with Memory
sessions.objectives: Introduce Screeps' Memory object; assign each creep a role/state/target using AI-assisted code.
sessions.liveActivity: Add per-creep Memory (role, state, target) with AI help; observe how state persists across ticks.
sessions.homework: Sketch what states each creep role can be in.

sessions.name: Session 5: Watch It Run, Then Describe It
sessions.objectives: Practice describing bugs in natural language instead of pasting raw errors; watch how much better AI fixes are when the description is clear.
sessions.liveActivity: Each student narrates a failure from their bot's run to the class, then converts it into a prompt for their AI agent.
sessions.homework: Write a one-paragraph bug report in plain English for the AI, no code pasted.

sessions.name: Session 6: Project Checkpoint - Explorer Badge
sessions.objectives: Ship a stable single-room economy bot; explain what it does in plain terms.
sessions.liveActivity: Demo day: each student shows their working bot surviving a full day, and explains it to a partner.
sessions.homework: Record a 2-minute walkthrough video of the bot for your portfolio.

programs.name: Understanding Generated Code
programs.description: Go back through the Program 1 Screeps bot and break down the architecture piece by piece: the tick loop, Memory persistence, role modules, and spawn logic. Students learn to read code they didn't originally write.
programs.learningOutcomes: Read and explain AI-generated code; understand Screeps' tick-based execution model and persistent Memory object; identify module boundaries.
programs.projectSummary: Rebuild one role module (e.g. the harvester) from scratch, unassisted, then compare it to the AI's original version.
programs.portfolioArtifact: A written/diagrammed architecture breakdown of their own bot.

sessions.name: Session 1: The Tick Loop, Explained
sessions.objectives: Introduce the Screeps tick loop and how it differs from a normal program's execution model.
sessions.liveActivity: Walk through main.js from Program 1's bot together, tracing exactly what runs each tick.
sessions.homework: Annotate your own main.js loop line-by-line in plain English.

sessions.name: Session 2: Memory as Persistent State
sessions.objectives: Understand why Screeps needs persistent Memory and how it's structured/serialized.
sessions.liveActivity: Inspect Memory in the Screeps console; deliberately clear it and observe what breaks.
sessions.homework: Diagram the current Memory schema of your bot (roles, states, targets).

sessions.name: Session 3: Finding the Module Boundaries
sessions.objectives: Identify the module boundaries the AI created (role files, utility files, dispatch logic).
sessions.liveActivity: Students trace a single creep's full lifecycle across every file it touches.
sessions.homework: List every function in your codebase and what triggers it.

sessions.name: Session 4: Naming What You See
sessions.objectives: Practice naming architecture patterns out loud, not just recognizing them silently.
sessions.liveActivity: Pair up and explain your bot's architecture to a partner using only whiteboard/paper, no code.
sessions.homework: Name three design decisions the AI made that you wouldn't have thought of.

sessions.name: Session 5: Rebuild It Yourself
sessions.objectives: Rebuild one module without AI assistance to test true understanding.
sessions.liveActivity: Students write the harvester role from scratch, unassisted, referencing only their own architecture diagram.
sessions.homework: Finish the manual rewrite of the harvester role if not done in session.

sessions.name: Session 6: Project Checkpoint - Builder Badge
sessions.objectives: Compare the manual rewrite to the AI's original version and articulate the differences.
sessions.liveActivity: Present your architecture breakdown diagram and manual rewrite to the group; discuss what surprised you.
sessions.homework: Submit your architecture diagram to the portfolio folder.

programs.name: Git, APIs & Databases
programs.description: Put the Screeps bot under real version control and deployment tooling: Git history, the Screeps API/CLI deploy pipeline, and Memory-as-a-database schema design.
programs.learningOutcomes: Use Git branches and commits meaningfully; deploy code via the Screeps CLI/API; design a simple data schema; use a diff tool to review AI-suggested changes before deploying.
programs.projectSummary: Bot code lives in Git, deploys via CLI, and pulls live stats into an external tracker via the Screeps API.
programs.portfolioArtifact: A Git repo with commit history + a live external stats dashboard.

sessions.name: Session 1: Git for a Living Codebase
sessions.objectives: Initialize Git for the bot codebase; learn commit hygiene and branching for experiments.
sessions.liveActivity: Turn the existing bot folder into a Git repo; make a branch to test a risky change safely.
sessions.homework: Make 3 small commits with meaningful messages before next session.

sessions.name: Session 2: Deploying via the Screeps API
sessions.objectives: Set up the Screeps CLI/API deploy pipeline (e.g. screeps-api/grunt-screeps).
sessions.liveActivity: Configure and run a CLI deploy; push a one-line change and confirm it takes effect live.
sessions.homework: Successfully deploy a small change to your live bot via CLI.

sessions.name: Session 3: Memory as a Database
sessions.objectives: Treat Memory as a lightweight database; design a clean schema for roles, room state, and task queues.
sessions.liveActivity: Redesign the bot's Memory schema as a small data model; implement it with AI assistance.
sessions.homework: Redesign your Memory schema on paper before touching code.

sessions.name: Session 4: External Stats Dashboard
sessions.objectives: Pull live Screeps stats via the API into an external dashboard (tie-in to Airtable or a webhook).
sessions.liveActivity: Build a simple external tracker pulling energy/creep-count stats from the Screeps API.
sessions.homework: Add one new stat to your external dashboard.

sessions.name: Session 5: Reviewing Before Deploying
sessions.objectives: Use a diff tool to review AI-suggested changes critically before deploying to a live bot.
sessions.liveActivity: Ask the AI agent for a refactor; review the diff line-by-line before approving the deploy.
sessions.homework: Review an AI-suggested diff and write one sentence on whether to accept it and why.

sessions.name: Session 6: Project Checkpoint - Developer Badge
sessions.objectives: Confirm the full pipeline works end-to-end: Git, CLI deploy, live bot, external stats.
sessions.liveActivity: Demo the full pipeline live: commit, deploy, and show the dashboard updating in real time.
sessions.homework: Share your Git repo link and dashboard screenshot in the portfolio folder.

programs.name: Software Architecture Patterns
programs.description: Scale the bot from one room to a multi-room empire, formalizing state machines, task-queue patterns, and manager/worker architecture — then predicting structure changes before asking AI to build them.
programs.learningOutcomes: Recognize and name recurring architecture patterns (state machine, task queue, manager/worker); predict structural changes needed for a new requirement before writing a prompt.
programs.projectSummary: Multi-room Screeps bot using a central task queue and manager modules (spawn manager, defense manager, expansion manager).
programs.portfolioArtifact: A multi-room bot with a documented manager/worker architecture diagram.

sessions.name: Session 1: Formalizing State Machines
sessions.objectives: Formalize creep behavior as an explicit state machine (harvesting to returning to idle) instead of implicit if/else logic.
sessions.liveActivity: Refactor existing creep logic into an explicit state machine with AI assistance; compare readability before/after.
sessions.homework: Diagram your creeps' current states and transitions.

sessions.name: Session 2: The Task Queue Pattern
sessions.objectives: Introduce the task-queue pattern: a central list of jobs that creeps pull from instead of hardcoded roles.
sessions.liveActivity: Implement a basic task queue and have idle creeps pull the next available job.
sessions.homework: List 3 tasks your bot currently hardcodes that could instead be queued.

sessions.name: Session 3: Manager/Worker Pattern
sessions.objectives: Introduce manager/worker architecture: spawn manager, defense manager, expansion manager as separate concerns.
sessions.liveActivity: Split monolithic logic into a spawn manager and a defense manager module with AI assistance.
sessions.homework: Sketch your manager modules and what each one owns.

sessions.name: Session 4: Scaling to Multiple Rooms
sessions.objectives: Identify what architecture changes are needed to manage more than one room (shared memory, room roles).
sessions.liveActivity: Claim a second room and adapt the manager architecture to coordinate across both rooms.
sessions.homework: Write the requirements for claiming a second room, in plain English.

sessions.name: Session 5: Predict Before You Prompt
sessions.objectives: Practice predicting architecture changes before writing the prompt that implements them.
sessions.liveActivity: Given a new requirement ('add remote mining'), students write their predicted architecture change, then compare it to what the AI actually builds.
sessions.homework: Predict, in writing, what modules a 'remote mining' feature would touch before next session's build.

sessions.name: Session 6: Project Checkpoint - Studio Member Badge
sessions.objectives: Confirm a working multi-room bot with modular manager architecture.
sessions.liveActivity: Demo the multi-room bot; present the manager/worker diagram and explain each module's responsibility.
sessions.homework: Submit your manager/worker architecture diagram to the portfolio folder.

programs.name: Debugging Systems
programs.description: Real debugging under real constraints — Screeps enforces a hard per-tick CPU budget, which forces students to profile, optimize, and handle edge cases (creep death, spawn queue overflow, a scripted NPC invader attacking the room) instead of just adding more code. All threats are automated/scripted opponents on the private server, not other students.
programs.learningOutcomes: Read console errors and visual overlays; profile and stay under a CPU budget; handle edge cases defensively; write clear bug reports/prompts instead of pasting raw errors at the AI.
programs.projectSummary: Bot survives a simulated attack or resource crisis, profiled and optimized to stay within its CPU limit.
programs.portfolioArtifact: A bot that survives a simulated stress scenario without crashing or exceeding its CPU budget.

sessions.name: Session 1: Reading Errors Like a Developer
sessions.objectives: Learn to read Screeps console errors and visual overlays; set up VS Code for local development.
sessions.liveActivity: Deliberately break a working script, then use console errors and overlays to locate the fault.
sessions.homework: Screenshot one console error from your own bot and bring it next session.

sessions.name: Session 2: CPU Budgeting as a Forcing Function
sessions.objectives: Understand Screeps' hard per-tick CPU budget and why it forces efficient code.
sessions.liveActivity: Profile the bot's CPU usage; identify the most expensive function and optimize it.
sessions.homework: Profile your bot and note its average CPU usage per tick.

sessions.name: Session 3: Catching Regressions in Diffs
sessions.objectives: Use diff-checking to catch regressions introduced by AI suggestions before they go live.
sessions.liveActivity: Ask the AI for an 'optimization'; diff it against the working version and hunt for a hidden regression.
sessions.homework: Practice writing one accept/reject note on an AI diff before next session.

sessions.name: Session 4: Defensive Coding for Edge Cases
sessions.objectives: Handle edge cases defensively: creep death, spawn queue overflow, a scripted NPC invader entering the room.
sessions.liveActivity: Simulate a creep dying mid-task and a spawn queue overflow; spawn a scripted NPC invader bot into the room and patch defenses against it.
sessions.homework: List 3 edge cases your bot doesn't currently handle.

sessions.name: Session 5: Writing Bug Reports the AI Can Act On
sessions.objectives: Practice directing an AI agent to fix a bug using a clear written description instead of pasted stack traces.
sessions.liveActivity: Trade bug reports with a partner and have each person's AI agent fix the other's issue from the written description alone.
sessions.homework: Write a clean bug report (no raw error paste) for a real issue in your bot.

sessions.name: Session 6: Project Checkpoint - Lead Developer Badge
sessions.objectives: Confirm the bot survives a stress scenario without crashing or exceeding its CPU budget.
sessions.liveActivity: Spawn a scripted NPC invader bot (or a resource crisis) against the bot; verify it survives within its CPU limit.
sessions.homework: Record your bot's CPU graph before and after this program for your portfolio.

programs.name: Build Your Own Application
programs.description: Capstone: design and build an original system solo — market trading, defense against scripted NPC invaders, automated expansion, or (for students who want to branch) a non-Screeps application — using everything from Programs 1-5, then present it like a studio review. Strong capstones become candidates for the AutoNateAI Screeps League.
programs.learningOutcomes: Independently design an architecture before coding it; execute a build sprint with AI assistance; give and receive peer code review; present technical decisions clearly.
programs.projectSummary: Capstone project: an original system built and presented solo, chosen and architected by the student.
programs.portfolioArtifact: An original, working system the student designed, built, and can explain end-to-end.

sessions.name: Session 1: Capstone Proposal
sessions.objectives: Choose and scope an original capstone system (market trading, defense against scripted NPC invaders, automated expansion, or a non-Screeps app).
sessions.liveActivity: Each student pitches their capstone idea and gets feedback before writing a line of code.
sessions.homework: Finalize your one-page architecture design doc.

sessions.name: Session 2: Architecture Design Doc
sessions.objectives: Write the architecture plan before touching the AI agent: modules, data flow, state, edge cases.
sessions.liveActivity: Peer-review each other's design docs before any building begins.
sessions.homework: Complete your architecture design doc if unfinished.

sessions.name: Session 3: Build Sprint 1
sessions.objectives: Implement the capstone's core system with AI assistance, following the design doc.
sessions.liveActivity: Working build session; instructor circulates for architecture-level guidance only, not code-level help.
sessions.homework: Push at least 3 commits toward the capstone build.

sessions.name: Session 4: Build Sprint 2
sessions.objectives: Iterate, debug, and optimize the capstone system toward a demoable state.
sessions.liveActivity: Second build session focused on debugging and polish, applying Program 5 skills.
sessions.homework: Have the capstone in a demoable state before next session.

sessions.name: Session 5: Peer Playtesting and Review
sessions.objectives: Give and receive structured peer review, mirroring a real studio code review.
sessions.liveActivity: Students test and review each other's capstone systems and leave written feedback.
sessions.homework: Incorporate at least one piece of peer feedback before showcase.

sessions.name: Session 6: Capstone Showcase - AI Software Architect Certificate
sessions.objectives: Present the working system and explain the architecture decisions behind it.
sessions.liveActivity: Final showcase: each student presents their original system live and fields questions from the group.
sessions.homework: Submit final capstone and write-up to the portfolio folder.

product.title: AutoNateAI Screeps League
product.cta: Enter your bot in the next AutoNateAI Screeps League

season.name: League Season 1 — Capture the Flag
season.winCondition: Capture the Flag (v1, borrowed from Screeps: Arena's simplest arena). Each side has one flag that cannot be destroyed or moved. First creep to touch the enemy's flag wins instantly. If time expires with neither flag touched, the match is a draw. No economy scoring, no judgment calls — just a position check. Future seasons can layer in economy (Spawn and Swamp style) or area-control scoring (Collect and Control style) once there's real gameplay data.
